title: Federal Open Market Committee announces approval of updates to its Statement on Longer-Run Goals and Monetary Policy Strategy
summary: The FOMC unanimously approved updates to its Statement on Longer-Run Goals and Monetary Policy Strategy on August 22, 2025. This framework statement, originally adopted in 2012 and previously reviewed in 2019-20, reflects lessons from the economy over the past five years. The Committee's two percent longer-run inflation goal was explicitly not a focus of the review.
key_points: The FOMC unanimously approved updates to its monetary policy framework statement
The framework statement was originally adopted in 2012 with a previous review in 2019-20
The 2025 review was announced in November 2024 and included Fed Listens events at Reserve Banks and a research conference in May 2025
Updates are intended to enhance transparency, accountability, and effectiveness of monetary policy
The Committee's two percent longer-run inflation goal was not a focus of the review
The statement emphasizes promoting maximum employment and stable prices for the benefit of all Americans
why_it_matters: The why_it_matters correctly notes that 'the source does not specify what changes were made'—this is accurate and appropriately caveats the record.
macro_context: This is the Fed's periodic review of its monetary policy framework, intended to ensure its approach remains appropriate for achieving its congressional mandates of maximum employment and stable prices.

issues_found: event_type 'monetary_policy_report' is borderline; this is a press release announcing framework approval, not a full report—the more precise type would be 'fomc_statement' or similar
Source is inherently thin—announces the update but does not specify what changes were made to the framework statement
The actual framework changes are in a linked PDF not included in this source, limiting archival value for substantive content
No specific policy signals or forward guidance language provided in this source

human_review.notes: The record is factually accurate and source attribution is correct, but the event_type may be imprecise and the source is thin with no substantive content on actual framework changes. Human review needed to assess if 'monetary_policy_report' is the correct event_type or if this should be reclassified as something like 'fomc_statement'. Also note the source was published 221 days before TODAY (well past 90-day threshold)—acceptable as historical policy document but worth flagging.

notes: Low-detail press release. Provides metadata about the framework review process and confirms unanimous approval and no change to the 2% inflation target, but does not articulate what specific changes were made to the framework statement. For substantive content, refer to linked PDF document.
